content:
pchickey opened issue #13168:

TK: @pchickey owes a better description here, but I need an issue number first

pchickey edited issue #13168:

In #13167 I introduced a test of using Wizer on a component compiled from Rust sources, adapted to wasip2 using the adapter (specifically by the test-programs-artifacts build.rs).

The wizer-rewritten component fails validation (here) when Wizer is run with the default settings or with keep_init_func(true), with the error message core instance 2 has no export named wizer-initialize (at offset 0xea441a)

It appears that the rewritten component still contains aliases to wizer-initialize at the outermost component. Snippet from the tail end of the component here shows an alias at the outermost (alias core export $main "wizer-initialize" (core func $wizer-initialize (;45;))) ($main is the core instance 2 reported above) and I suspect some off-by-one errors below that first invalid entry, where an export of "run" refers to $"#func17 wizer-initialize".

<details>
<pre>
(core instance $fixup-args (;16;)
(export "$imports" (table $"shim table"))
(export "0" (func $random_get))
(export "1" (func $environ_get))
(export "2" (func $environ_sizes_get))
(export "3" (func $fd_write))
(export "4" (func $proc_exit))
(export "5" (func $"#core-func33 indirect-wasi:filesystem/types@0.2.6-filesystem-error-code"))
(export "6" (func $"#core-func34 indirect-wasi:filesystem/types@0.2.6-[method]descriptor.get-type"))
(export "7" (func $"#core-func35 indirect-wasi:filesystem/types@0.2.6-[method]descriptor.append-via-stream"))
(export "8" (func $"#core-func36 indirect-wasi:filesystem/types@0.2.6-[method]descriptor.write-via-stream"))
(export "9" (func $"#core-func37 indirect-wasi:filesystem/types@0.2.6-[method]descriptor.stat"))
(export "10" (func $"#core-func38 indirect-wasi:filesystem/preopens@0.2.6-get-directories"))
(export "11" (func $"#core-func39 indirect-wasi:cli/environment@0.2.6-get-environment"))
(export "12" (func $"#core-func40 indirect-wasi:io/streams@0.2.6-[method]output-stream.check-write"))
(export "13" (func $"#core-func41 indirect-wasi:io/streams@0.2.6-[method]output-stream.blocking-flush"))
(export "14" (func $"#core-func42 indirect-wasi:io/streams@0.2.6-[method]output-stream.blocking-write-and-flush"))
(export "15" (func $"#core-func43 indirect-wasi:io/streams@0.2.6-[method]output-stream.write"))
(export "16" (func $"#core-func44 indirect-wasi:random/random@0.2.6-get-random-bytes"))
)
(core instance $fixup (;17;) (instantiate $wit-component-fixup
(with "" (instance $fixup-args))
)
)
(type (;23;) (func))
(alias core export $main "wizer-initialize" (core func $wizer-initialize (;45;)))
(func $wizer-initialize (;16;) (type 23) (canon lift (core func $wizer-initialize)))
(type (;24;) (func (param "arg" s32) (result s32)))
(alias core export $main "run" (core func $run (;46;)))
(func $"#func17 wizer-initialize" (@name "wizer-initialize") (;17;) (type 24) (canon lift (core func $run)))
(export $run (;18;) "run" (func $"#func17 wizer-initialize"))
(type (;25;) (result))
(type (;26;) (func (result 25)))
(alias core export $"#core-instance15 wasi_snapshot_preview1" "wasi:cli/run@0.2.6#run" (core func $wasi:cli/run@0.2.6#run (;47;)))
(func $"#func19 run" (@name "run") (;19;) (type 26) (canon lift (core func $wasi:cli/run@0.2.6#run)))
(component $wasi:cli/run@0.2.6-shim-component (;0;)
(type (;0;) (result))
(type (;1;) (func (result 0)))
(import "import-func-run" (func (;0;) (type 1)))
(type (;2;) (result))
(type (;3;) (func (result 2)))
(export (;1;) "run" (func 0) (func (type 3)))
)
(instance $wasi:cli/run@0.2.6-shim-instance (;11;) (instantiate $wasi:cli/run@0.2.6-shim-component
(with "import-func-run" (func $"#func19 run"))
)
)
(export $wasi:cli/run@0.2.6 (;12;) "wasi:cli/run@0.2.6" (instance $wasi:cli/run@0.2.6-shim-instance))
(@producers
(processed-by "wit-component" "0.247.0")
)
)
</pre>
</details>

The wizer-rewritten component fails validation (here) when Wizer is run with the default settings or with keep_init_func(true), with the error message core instance 2 has no export named wizer-initialize (at offset 0xea441a)

It appears that the rewritten component still contains aliases to wizer-initialize at the outermost component. Snippet from the tail end of the component here shows an alias at the outermost (alias core export $main "wizer-initialize" (core func $wizer-initialize (;45;))) ($main is the core instance 2 reported above) and I suspect some off-by-one errors below that first invalid entry, where an export of "run" refers to $"#func17 wizer-initialize".

pchickey edited issue #13168:

In #13167 I introduced a test of using Wizer on a component compiled from Rust sources, adapted to wasip2 using the adapter (specifically by the test-programs-artifacts build.rs).

The wizer-rewritten component fails validation (here) when Wizer is run with the default settings or with keep_init_func(true), with the error message core instance 2 has no export named wizer-initialize (at offset 0xea441a)

It appears that the rewritten component still contains aliases to wizer-initialize at the outermost component. Snippet from the tail end of the component here shows an alias at the outermost (alias core export $main "wizer-initialize" (core func $wizer-initialize (;45;))) ($main is the core instance 2 reported above) and I suspect some off-by-one errors below that first invalid entry, where an export of "run" refers to $"#func17 wizer-initialize".

For now, using wizer with components with keep_init_func(false) otherwise appears to be working.

pchickey edited issue #13168:

In #13167 I introduced a test of using Wizer on a component compiled from Rust sources, adapted to wasip2 using the adapter (specifically by the test-programs-artifacts build.rs).

Using wizer with components with keep_init_func(false) appears to be working.

The wizer-rewritten component fails validation (here) when Wizer is run with the default settings or with keep_init_func(true), with the error message core instance 2 has no export named wizer-initialize (at offset 0xea441a)

It appears that the rewritten component still contains aliases to wizer-initialize at the outermost component. Snippet from the tail end of the component here shows an alias at the outermost (alias core export $main "wizer-initialize" (core func $wizer-initialize (;45;))) ($main is the core instance 2 reported above) and I suspect some off-by-one errors below that first invalid entry, where an export of "run" refers to $"#func17 wizer-initialize".

The failure can be reproduced by modifying https://github.com/bytecodealliance/wasmtime/blob/eb7a3f61290ac9e82fe7d760fb569bb3f3fc657a/crates/wizer/tests/all/component.rs#L694
